mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or t-statistic for trigger effect is < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in real retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between retail sales and housing starts yields an insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample predictive regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power or reverses direction during periods when the federal funds rate increases by more than 25 basis points per meeting.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.5 percent year-over-year threshold, persistent inflation expectations force monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPI exceeds the threshold, with a hit rate below 40% for negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal of rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market health, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models, with interaction IC < 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows, or 60-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with GDP growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production indicate a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of cases following the trigger, invalidating the contraction signal.
